div.h2: Signal Delivery in Traced Processes — Suppress vs Re-inject

g.text: Signals That Break Programs If Suppressed
SIGALRM | 14 | alarm() / setitimer() timeouts — never fire
SIGTERM | 15 | Graceful shutdown — process never terminates cleanly
SIGCHLD | 17 | Parent never learns child exited — wait() hangs
SIGHUP | 1 | Daemon config reload — never triggered
SIGPIPE | 13 | Write to closed pipe — EPIPE never delivered
SIGUSR1 | 10 | App-defined signal — custom handler never runs
SIGWINCH | 28 | Terminal resize — UI never redraws
NOTE: suppress ONLY ptrace-synthetic SIGTRAPs (initial exec stop)
Stop Type | sig value | Resume signal arg
Initial exec stop | SIGTRAP, event==0 | 0 (no signal)

text.tspan: // PTRACE_EVENT stop — always resume with signal=0
// Syscall stop (PTRACE_O_TRACESYSGOOD) — no signal to deliver
// Initial exec stop or breakpoint — no signal to deliver
// PTRACE_EVENT stop — always resume with signal=0
// Syscall stop (PTRACE_O_TRACESYSGOOD) — no signal to deliver
// Initial exec stop or breakpoint — no signal to deliver
